intro: Malleola cladophylax, also known as the Malleola orchid, is a rare and endangered epiphytic orchid native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, thanks to its health benefits which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Malleola cladophylax include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
